sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Technology Approach Classification
required_elements: Quantum computing approach classification (superconducting, trapped ion, photonic, neutral atom, topological, software/algorithms)
Technology readiness level (TRL) assessment (basic research, proof of concept, prototype, commercial-ready)
Honest TRL positioning relative to industry state (acknowledge limited commercial-ready systems as of 2026)

sections.title: Technical Benchmarking — Cutting Through the Qubit Hype
required_elements: Logical qubits demonstrated and error rate analysis (vs. physical qubits hype)
Quantum Volume / CLOPS (Circuit Layer Operations Per Second) metrics
Error rates (single-qubit gate, two-qubit gate, measurement fidelity)
Coherence time and connectivity/topology assessment (full vs. nearest-neighbor connectivity)

sections.title: Path to Commercial Value — The 'Quantum Advantage' Timeline
required_elements: NISQ-era applications (2024-2028) timeline and commercial viability assessment
Early fault-tolerant era (2028-2032) revenue emergence probability
Full fault-tolerant era (2032+) timeline assessment
Revenue bridge assessment (government contracts, quantum-as-a-service, consulting, classical products)
Cash burn rate and runway analysis

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Probability-weighted scenario analysis (milestone achievement probabilities, revenue outcomes)
Cash burn runway analysis (months of cash at current burn rate)
Optionality-based valuation (company value as option premium on quantum advantage)
Implied TAM capture analysis (% of projected market embedded in current market cap)

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Qubit count announcements without error rate improvements (metric gaming)
Revenue guidance based on 'quantum-inspired' classical algorithms vs. actual quantum
Key scientist departures (talent moat erosion)
Government contract dependence exceeding 80% (policy risk)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Independent technical benchmarking against peer systems
Government contract pipeline analysis (DARPA/DOE/DOD quantum programs)
Customer/partner validation (pharma, financial services running production workloads)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Technology approach clearly identified with specific implementation type
TRL explicitly stated with honest assessment of 2026 commercial-readiness landscape
Logical qubit count and error rates documented (not physical qubit marketing numbers)
Quantum Volume or CLOPS metrics compared to peer systems if available
Error rate roadmap to fault-tolerant quantum (<10^-3 two-qubit gate fidelity target) documented
Timeline for NISQ applications, early fault-tolerant, and full fault-tolerant eras specified
Revenue bridge mechanism identified (government contracts %, quantum-as-a-service, other)
Cash burn rate calculated with runway in months at current burn
Implied TAM capture quantified from current market cap
Government contract percentage calculated (if >80%, policy risk flagged)
Investigation tracks include independent NIST/DOE benchmark data, DARPA contract awards, and customer validation sources
